title: Bounties Generated by Faction
query: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(Fine), 0) AS total FROM CommitCrime WHERE Fine > 0 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT Faction AS label, COALESCE(SUM(Fine), 0) AS value FROM CommitCrime WHERE Fine > 0 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY Faction ORDER BY value DESC

details.text: {total} Cr in bounties generated across all faction jurisdictions during the report period. A large bounty total means you've been very busy, very reckless, or both — the faction breakdown reveals which jurisdictions have the biggest active price on your head and which authorities you've antagonised most.
details.help: CommitCrime Fine is non-zero for crimes that generate bounties. total drives the title as the total financial liability incurred.

tagLines: Bounty generation log compiled. Faction jurisdiction liability totals noted in the security record.
Every bounty is a price on your head in someone's jurisdiction. Yours add up.
The factions don't forget. That number is what clearing your record is going to cost.
